title: Resident Evil 4
subtitle: Remake (the video game)
review: Eschewing the slow maze and puzzle style of the first couple of games, and embracing action more confidently than the remake of {Resident Evil 3}, it might not be everyone's favourite style of Resi game, but it's undoubtedly thrilling and fun. Leon is powerful and agile, and is fed a constant stream of ammunition and resources to play with. You still have to pay close attention to the contents your attaché case, but not so much that you are forced to run past enemies without engaging them in combat, like in his previous mission in Raccoon City. In {Resident Evil 2} zombies don't drop items when they die, so shooting them strictly consumes ammo, while shooting enemies in this game is always worth it because of the loot they drop. At times it feels like you're playing as Indiana Jones, and yet it can it can make you panic and run in a way that no movie action hero would. There are so many weapons that you'd never use them all in a single playthrough, so there's an inherent reward for playing again and choosing a different build, without the game having to rely on bonus modes or recycled content. There is a surprising amount of variation in the gameplay, for a survival horror game. I loved it, and I was very impressed with how polished, beautiful, stylish and well-optimised it was. The music is also great, especially during boss battles.
